name: Flown-away Birds
intro: They are called ‘ucelli scappati’ (which means ‘flown-away birds’) because the combination of the pancetta, veal and liver apparently tastes like the wild birds that were once used.

ingredients.item: calf’s liver, sliced about 2 cm thick
veal nut (noix, or topside), sliced about 2 cm thick
chicken or veal stock for moisture, if needed

method: Cut the liver, veal and pancetta into square cubes. Dust the liver and veal with flour. Then, alternating, thread the liver, sage, veal and pancetta on skewers.
Take a pan large enough to hold all the skewers at once and, in the butter and oil, brown the skewers of meat one or two at a time on all sides, then set aside in a dish.

Add the garlic to the pan with a few more sage leaves. Let the garlic become golden (do not burn it) then add the wine and scrape up all the bits in the bottom of the pan. When it bubbles add the skewers, salt and pepper and let them cook, covered and on low heat, for about 20 minutes.
If the dish seems too dry before cooking is finished, add a bit of veal or chicken stock.
